title: Source-to-Package Release, Incident Command, and Production Defense · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in defensible production TypeScript feed aggregator and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources through authorized discovery, bounded Fetch and XML, runtime normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage publisher overload, stale ingestion, SSRF, entity, sanitizer, search, pool, tenant, async-handle, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Release Provenance
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 6554 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend source-to-package provenance, unfamiliar end-to-end behavior, incidents, recovery, and residual risks; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.

steps.instruction:
